text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Clinical Trial Visit — Cycle 4 Completion / Response Assessment

ATTENDING: [Thoracic Oncology]
VISIT TYPE: Established patient, clinical trial follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Ms. Tran is a 64-year-old Vietnamese-American woman with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (right lower lobe primary, T790M acquired resistance post-Tagrisso, prior SRS to brain metastases) presenting for cycle 4 completion assessment and response review on protocol: investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody (JBI-602-like construct) IV every 2 weeks plus oral SOS1 inhibitor daily (BI 1701963 analog), 28-day cycles.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Tran is now approximately 2 years out from her initial diagnosis of stage IIIB (T3N2M0) lung adenocarcinoma. She was originally treated with Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as first-line therapy, achieving a sustained partial response for approximately 14 months before developing radiographic progression — enlarging right lower lobe primary (3.1 cm), new right adrenal metastasis, new bilateral pulmonary nodules, recurrent mediastinal adenopathy at 4R, and three new brain metastases (right cerebellar, left frontal, right parietal). Repeat biopsy of the adrenal lesion confirmed metastatic lung adenocarcinoma, and NGS revealed acquired EGFR T790M as the dominant resistance mechanism, with subclonal PIK3CA E545K and low-level CDK4 amplification. She underwent Gamma Knife SRS to the three intracranial lesions (all treated with single-fraction dosing, well-tolerated), then transitioned to Paraplatin AUC 5 plus pem (Alimta) 500 mg/m2 every 21 days as second-line systemic therapy. carbo was dose-reduced to AUC 4 after cycle 2 due to grade 3 thrombocytopenia (platelet nadir 42K) requiring a platelet transfusion. She completed 4 cycles of doublet therapy with partial response on restaging CT (primary down to 2.4 cm from 3.1 cm). She then transitioned to Alimta maintenance for 2 cycles, during which time she underwent clinical trial screening. peme was discontinued upon trial enrollment.

She initiated the current protocol approximately 4 cycles ago (approximately 16 weeks of trial treatment). Cycle 1 Day 1 was uneventful. On Cycle 1 Day 15, she had a grade 2 infusion-related reaction (flushing, urticaria, transient hypotension to 88/52 mmHg) which was managed with IV diphenhydramine and hydrocortisone, resolved within 20 minutes, and the infusion was completed at reduced rate. Pre-medications were enhanced for all subsequent infusions and she has tolerated cycles 2, 3, and 4 infusions without further infusion reactions. She has had re-emergence of EGFR-related acneiform rash, grade 1-2, managed with topical clindamycin gel. Restaging imaging after 2 cycles demonstrated encouraging partial response (right lower lobe mass 1.7 cm from 2.3 cm, adrenal met down to 0.7 cm from 1.1 cm, bilateral pulmonary nodules resolved, brain lesions with continued treatment response/radiation necrosis, no new CNS disease).

Today the patient presents for her cycle 4 completion visit and 4-month protocol-specified response assessment. Overall she reports feeling significantly better compared to the period around her progression — dyspnea has resolved, energy has gradually improved, and she has been able to return to part-time teaching at the high school level. She denies new headaches, focal neurological symptoms, visual changes, or seizures. She does report intermittent grade 1 nausea associated with the oral SOS1 inhibitor, which she manages with ondansetron as needed and which has not required dose modification. Residual fingertip paresthesias from prior carbo remain present and stable at grade 1 — she finds them a nuisance but not functionally limiting. The acneiform rash is mild and well-controlled on topical therapy.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Constitutional: No fever, chills, or significant weight loss. Fatigue markedly improved compared to the period of platinum doublet chemotherapy — now grade 0-1 at baseline. Appetite good.
Respiratory: Dyspnea resolved. No cough. No hemoptysis.
Neurological: No new headaches, no dizziness, no focal weakness, no vision changes, no seizure activity. Fingertip paresthesias stable (grade 1, bilateral).
Gastrointestinal: Intermittent mild nausea (grade 1), managed with ondansetron. No vomiting. Bowel habits regular.
Dermatologic: Mild acneiform eruption face/neck, well-controlled on topical clindamycin.
Musculoskeletal: No bone pain, no new joint complaints.
Cardiovascular: No palpitations, no chest pain, no edema.
Endocrine: No symptoms of thyroid dysfunction. Levothyroxine continued unchanged.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: BP 122/76 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 16, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 58.2 kg (stable, +0.4 kg from last visit), Temperature 36.8°C.
General: Well-appearing Asian woman, appears stated age, in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Good affect, engaged in discussion.
HEENT: Scattered papulopustular lesions on bilateral cheeks and chin, consistent with grade 1 acneiform rash; no involvement of scalp or periorbital region. PERRLA, EOMI. Oropharynx clear; no mucositis.
Neck: Supple, no cervical lymphadenopathy. No thyromegaly.
Chest: Decreased breath sounds right base with mild dullness to percussion (chronic, compared to baseline); no crackles, no wheeze. No rhonchi.
Cardiac: Regular rate and rhythm, S1/S2 without murmur. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. Right flank — no palpable mass.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Nail changes: right index and middle fingernails with mild residual paronychia changes (healed, no active inflammation). Fingertips with diminished sensation to light touch bilaterally (grade 1 peripheral neuropathy).
Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Cerebellar exam — finger-nose-finger intact, no dysmetria. Tandem gait normal. Romberg negative. No focal deficits.
Lymph nodes: No supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal adenopathy.
Skin: Grade 1 acneiform rash as above. No other rashes, no petechiae, no purpura.
ECOG Performance Status: 1.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained within 7 days per protocol):
CBC: WBC 6.8 K/uL (normal differential), Hgb 11.4 g/dL (stable mild anemia, improved from treatment nadir), Plt 198 K/uL (normalized). ANC 3.9 K/uL.
CMP: Na 138, K 4.1, Cl 101, CO2 26, BUN 14, Cr 0.82 mg/dL (CrCl estimated 74 mL/min — adequate). Glucose 98.
LFTs: AST 22 U/L, ALT 18 U/L, ALP 67 U/L, Total Bili 0.6 mg/dL, Albumin 3.9 g/dL. LFTs normal — no hepatotoxicity.
Coags: INR 1.0.
Thyroid: TSH 2.8 mIU/L (well-controlled on levothyroxine 88 mcg daily).
Magnesium: 1.9 mEq/L (low-normal; oral supplementation noted in pharmacy record).
EKG (done at cycle 3): QTc 438 ms (within normal range).
Note: B12 injection (1000 mcg IM) administered per Alimta maintenance supplementation protocol — patient and team confirmed this is to be continued per trial protocol for patients previously on Alimta. Folic acid 400 mcg daily also ongoing.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (4-month protocol timepoint): Right lower lobe mass continues to show interval decrease, now measuring approximately 1.4 cm — down from 1.7 cm at the 2-month scan and 2.3 cm at trial enrollment. The mass retains soft tissue density with stable spiculated margins; no cavitation. Right adrenal nodule essentially stable at approximately 0.6-0.7 cm, slightly smaller than prior. No new pulmonary nodules identified — the previously visualized bilateral nodules that had resolved remain absent. Previously noted mediastinal adenopathy at 4R has decreased further and is now at the lower limit of detection. No pleural effusion. No new liver, bone, or retroperitoneal disease.

MRI brain with and without gadolinium (4-month protocol timepoint): Previously treated right cerebellar, left frontal, and right parietal lesions demonstrate further volume reduction with internal signal characteristics consistent with maturing treatment effect and radiation necrosis. No evidence of active enhancing tumor at any of the three SRS sites. No new intracranial lesions. No leptomeningeal enhancement.

---

ASSESSMENT:
Ms. Tran is a 64-year-old woman with stage IV EGFR L858R/T790M-mutant lung adenocarcinoma who has now completed 4 cycles of protocol therapy (investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody plus oral SOS1 inhibitor) and is demonstrating a sustained and deepening partial response. The right lower lobe primary has gone from 2.3 cm at enrollment to approximately 1.4 cm — a continued, clinically meaningful reduction. The right adrenal metastasis remains essentially stable to slightly reduced. There is no new systemic disease and no new intracranial disease. Brain lesions treated with Gamma Knife continue to demonstrate excellent local control with radiation necrosis changes.

Tolerance to trial therapy is favorable. The grade 2 infusion-related reaction from cycle 1 has not recurred with enhanced pre-medication. EGFR-related rash is grade 1, well-managed topically. Nausea from the SOS1 inhibitor is grade 1, managed with PRN ondansetron. Residual grade 1 peripheral neuropathy from prior carbo is stable and non-limiting. Hematologic parameters have normalized following completion of platinum doublet therapy. Overall functional status is ECOG PS 1 with ongoing recovery of energy and the ability to maintain part-time occupational activity.

---

PLAN:
1. CLINICAL TRIAL CONTINUATION: Continue current protocol — investigational EGFR/MET bispecific antibody IV every 2 weeks plus oral SOS1 inhibitor daily. Patient is cycle 5 day 1 eligible today (all labs meet protocol thresholds, no dose modification criteria met).

2. INFUSION PRE-MEDICATIONS: Continue enhanced pre-medication protocol (cetirizine 10 mg PO night prior + diphenhydramine 25 mg IV + acetaminophen 650 mg PO + methylprednisolone 40 mg IV) for all bispecific antibody infusions given prior grade 2 IRR history.

3. EGFR RASH: Continue topical clindamycin 1% gel to affected areas. Counsel on sun protection and gentle skin care. Grade 1 — no systemic therapy modification warranted.

4. NAUSEA: Continue ondansetron 8 mg PO PRN before oral SOS1 inhibitor. If grade escalates, reassess dosing and consider scheduled antiemetic.

5. PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY: Monitor clinically. Grade 1, stable. No intervention at this time. If progression to grade 2 or functional impairment, reassess.

6. SUPPLEMENTATION: Continue folic acid 400 mcg daily and B12 1000 mcg IM every 9 weeks per protocol. Oral magnesium supplementation ongoing.

7. HYPOTHYROIDISM / HTN: Levothyroxine 88 mcg daily and amlodipine 5 mg daily continued unchanged. TSH well-controlled. Will recheck TSH at next visit in approximately 4 weeks.

8. NEXT VISIT: Cycle 5 infusion day 15 visit and labs in 2 weeks. Next protocol-specified restaging imaging at 6-month timepoint (approximately 2 months from today).

9. PATIENT COMMUNICATION: Reviewed imaging results and response with Ms. Tran and her daughter who accompanied her today. She was very encouraged by the continued response. The palliative intent of therapy and goals of care were readdressed in the context of ongoing good response. Patient remains fully informed and engaged. Emotional and psychological wellbeing appears stable; she finds continued teaching to be meaningful and therapeutic.

10. Cancer present, ongoing partial response on clinical trial therapy.

Time spent: approximately 45 minutes, greater than 50% in counseling and coordination of care.

Electronically signed: [Attending Physician, Thoracic Oncology]
